title: Best Restaurants in Hat Yai Thailand (2026)
meta_description: Where to eat in Hat Yai for 2026: TAT-backed fried chicken, dim sum, halal-friendly kitchens, Kim Yong morning food, Khlong Hae weekends, and strong hotel dining when convenience matters.
intro: Hat Yai food is better when you stop forcing it into one lane. The city's real strength is the mix: Thai-Chinese breakfast culture, Malay-Muslim and halal-friendly references, market grazing, fried chicken, and a few useful hotel restaurants for comfort and convenience. These are the Hat Yai food stops that actually fit the city.

items.name: Decha Fried Chicken & Seafood
items.description: One of the clearest TAT-backed Hat Yai food references, and a much stronger anchor than review-scraped 'best restaurant' lists.
highlights: Good first serious meal in Hat Yai
Fits the city's southern border-food identity
items.current_info: Best used as a real meal rather than as a token fried-chicken stop.

items.name: Kim Yong Market food lanes
items.description: The market-side answer to Hat Yai breakfast and snack culture if you want the city to feel local from the first hour.
highlights: Works with shopping and market browsing
Useful for snacks, tea, and takeaways
items.current_info: Go early and keep this as part of a broader market morning, not as your only food plan of the day.

items.name: Khlong Hae Floating Market
items.description: A worthwhile late-afternoon and evening food run when the official Friday-to-Sunday schedule lines up with your stay.
highlights: Food from boats and land stalls
items.current_info: This is a food-and-atmosphere stop, not a weekday fallback.

items.name: Rafa Dimsum Halal
items.description: A practical breakfast move from TAT's halal guide, useful if you want Hat Yai's dim sum side without generic cafe filler.
items.current_info: Works best early in the day before downtown walking or a Songkhla side trip.

items.name: Sami Kitchen
items.description: A useful TAT-listed local restaurant if you want a sit-down meal beyond markets and better-known downtown names.
highlights: Useful as a proper lunch or dinner stop
items.current_info: Best used when you are already in the Khlong Hae side of the city.

items.name: Salma Restaurant
items.description: A dependable city-center meal when you want local-food practicality more than atmosphere or branding.
items.current_info: A strong choice for a straightforward city-center lunch or dinner.

items.name: Ginger Restaurant at Centara Hotel Hat Yai
items.description: The right hotel-dining option when weather, timing, or city-center convenience matters more than chasing one more street-food lane.
highlights: Useful in bad weather or after a long travel day
items.current_info: Better as a convenience-led meal than as the whole reason to choose Hat Yai.

items.name: Signature Cafe & Restaurant
items.description: A solid official in-house option if your stay is on the outer-center or airport side and you want one easy meal at the hotel.
items.current_info: Most useful when hotel convenience is part of the plan, not when you are deep in the downtown market zone.
